title: Polypodium selliguea
taxonomy: Polypodium selliguea is a species of epiphytic fern in the family Polypodiaceae. This classification is based on the plant's growth habit and its placement within the larger group of vascular plants. The Polypodiaceae family is further classified under the division Pteridophyta, which includes all vascular plants that produce spores. The Pteridophyta division is part of the larger kingdom Plantae, encompassing a wide variety of plant species. The classification of Polypodium selliguea reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants within these taxonomic groups.

intro: Polypodium selliguea is a species of fern native to Australia and Southeast Asia, belonging to the Polypodiaceae family. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of anxiety, improve wound healing, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Polypodium selliguea include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, such as quercetin, kaempferol, and ursolic acid, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Polypodium selliguea include tea infusions, capsules, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
